task:
Write a complete IEEE-style research paper based on a literature study.

Title: "A Comprehensive Literature Review of Emerging Large Language Models"

Author Information:
Zain Ul Abidin
zainulabdin9952gmail.com
Department of Computer Systems Engineering
Sukkur IBA University, Sukkur, Pakistan

REQUIREMENTS:

* This is a pure literature review paper on recent and emerging large language models.
* Do NOT run any code. Do NOT include experiments or generated results.
* Base the paper entirely on existing research studies, published papers, and surveys.
* Provide a comprehensive and critical analysis of the evolution, architectures, and capabilities of modern large language models.

FORMAT:

* Write in single-column IEEE-style research paper format.
* Follow strict IEEE structure with these sections:
  Abstract, Keywords, I. Introduction, II. Related Work,
  III. Overview of Large Language Models,
  IV. Comparative Analysis,
  V. Challenges and Limitations,
  VI. Future Directions,
  VII. Conclusion, References

CONTENT REQUIREMENTS:

* Each section must be fully written with a minimum of 2 to 3 detailed paragraphs.
* Introduction and Related Work must contain at least 4 well-developed paragraphs each.
* Include discussion of major models such as transformer-based architectures, scaling laws, and training paradigms.
* Cover well-known LLM families and advancements in architecture, efficiency, and multimodality.
* Provide critical comparison across models in terms of performance, efficiency, and applicability.
* Include discussion on ethical concerns, bias, computational cost, and deployment challenges.

TABLES AND FIGURES:

* Include comparison tables summarizing different LLMs, their architectures, parameters, and key features.
* Include conceptual diagrams of transformer architecture and LLM pipelines where appropriate.
* Reference all figures properly in IEEE format such as Fig. 1, Fig. 2.
* Provide clear and professional captions for every figure and table.

WRITING STYLE:

* Do not use em dashes anywhere.
* Do not use bullet points inside section text. Write everything in formal paragraph form.
* Maintain academic tone, clarity, and technical depth.
* Avoid repetition and ensure logical flow across sections.

REFERENCES:

* Use IEEE citation style [1], [2], [3].
* Include at least 30 to 40 high-quality and recent references.
* Ensure no duplicate references.

OUTPUT QUALITY:

* Target length must be approximately 15 pages in single-column format.
* The paper must be comprehensive, well-structured, and publication-ready.
* Ensure strong critical analysis rather than simple description of models.
